Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for threads related to the Keiko Tanaka laser cutter shipment using search_emails with a query related to "Keiko Tanaka" or "laser cutter" or "placarding" (or similar). This should surface: (a) the original Mosaic 3-relocation request from tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com describing Keiko's Epilog Fusion Pro 48 laser cutter move from Columbus to Seattle; (b) Emeka Diallo's reply acknowledging the three relocations and forwarding to operations for detailed planning, noting the specialized equipment requirements; (c) Emeka Diallo's forward to chloe.vance@moveops.com requesting ops review and vendor planning for all three Mosaic relocations; (c2) Chloe Vance's reply to Emeka (email_id: mosaic_3reloc_chloe_ops_reply_001) confirming the vendor assignments and coordinator allocations specifically listing "Keiko Tanaka - Swift Relocations (long-haul + hazmat + storage), coordinated by Javier Morales"; (d) Chloe's URGENT email to greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com requesting the signed placarding certificate; (e) Greg Pallone's reply confirming the placarding was completed by driver Mike Becerra at the Columbus pickup on April 15, Swift job #SR-2026-04-1538, and that the signed cert, liability waiver, and Swift PHMSA registration #3281074 will be sent within 24 hours.
OE 2: Search emails for the DOT compliance inquiry and audit correspondence using search_emails with a query related to "DOT" or "PHMSA" or "hazmat audit" or "compliance inquiry" (or similar). This should surface: (a) the formal DOT inquiry letter from linda.castellano@dot.gov to marcus.thorne@moveops.com (Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187) requesting hazmat documentation for all MoveOps shipments Jan–Jun 2026, with a May 21, 2026 deadline; (b) Marcus's forward of the DOT letter to chloe.vance@moveops.com with instructions to inventory all hazmat moves; (c) Chloe's reply to Marcus identifying four in-scope moves: Mei-Lin Zhao (GreenStack, Class 9 lithium, Houston-Portland), Keiko Tanaka (Mosaic, Class 3B laser, Columbus-Seattle), Raj Patel (NorthWind, Class 9 lithium UPS, Chicago-Denver), and Joaquin Reyes (GreenStack, oversize rig, Phoenix-Seattle not hazmat but flagged due to overweight permit); (d) Marcus's extension request to Linda Castellano citing the missing placarding certificate gap and requesting a 15-day extension; (e) Linda Castellano's reply granting the extension to June 5, 2026; (f) Marcus's forward of the extension to Chloe with follow-up flags.
OE 3: Search Slack messages for discussions related to the DOT audit, hazmat documentation, or Keiko Tanaka placarding using conversations_search_messages with a query related to "DOT" or "hazmat" or "placarding" or "Keiko Tanaka" (or similar). This should surface: (a) Chloe's parent message in #operations (C006, ts: 1776889620.000000) posting the DOT hazmat documentation checklist template, the Airtable verification gate plan, and announcing a mandatory training session for Thursday April 24 at 2:00 PM Pacific this parent message references all four coordinators (Fatimah, Blessing, Javier, Suki) by name, references the Keiko Tanaka incident as a case study, and asks coordinators to review their in-progress moves for DOT-regulated materials; (b) Chloe's thread reply in #operations (ts: 1776891300.000000, thread_ts: 1776889620.000000) confirming the training invite was sent, re-tagging all four coordinators to accept the calendar invite, reiterating the Keiko Tanaka incident as a case study along with Marcus covering financial exposure, and reminding coordinators to review their in-progress moves for anything DOT-regulated before Thursday's session; (c) Hana Kim's message in #finance (C005, ts: 1776823140.000000, thread_ts: 1776795120.000000) about the $50,000 DOT penalty accrual (ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001) booked as a contingent liability under a new "Regulatory Compliance DOT Penalties (Pending)" account, linked to vendor U.S. DOT PHMSA, noting the Keiko Tanaka missing cert as the confirmed gap; (d) Marcus's message in #finance (C005, ts: 1777005240.000000, thread_ts: 1776795120.000000) about Linda Castellano granting the extension to June 5, 2026, the $50K accrual remaining unchanged, the risk of an expanded on-site inspection if the submission is incomplete, the requirement that all documentation must be signed originals or certified copies (unsigned drafts and verbal attestations will not be accepted), and separately that training records must meet 49 CFR 172.704(d) standards.
OE 4: Search or retrieve the Keiko Tanaka relocation record from Airtable using airtable_search_records (with base_id for the MoveOps base, table_name "Relocations", field_name "Name", and value "Keiko Tanaka") or airtable_list_records (with base_id and table_name "Relocations"). The agent should find record recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle (in table_id tblRelocations01) with: Name: Keiko Tanaka, Company: Mosaic Robotics, Status: In Progress, Origin: Columbus, OH, Destination: Seattle, WA, Move Start Date: 2026-04-15, Move End Date: 2026-04-23, Assigned Coordinator: Javier Morales, Special Requirements mentioning the Epilog Fusion Pro 48 laser cutter (400 lbs, ~$35K), Class 3B CO2 laser tube, DOT 49 CFR Part 173 hazmat placarding requirement, Swift crew handling, $800 hazmat surcharge, 8-day interim storage, and $450 storage fee.
OE 5: Search or list Linear issues related to the DOT compliance audit using linear_list_issues (with project "project_dot_compliance_audit" or query "DOT" or similar) to find the DOT audit tracking tickets. The agent should discover: (a) issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic (OPS-2, "DOT Docs - Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic / Class 3B Laser"), state: in_progress, assignee: Chloe Vance - this is the main ticket tracking the placarding gap. Its description notes Blessing Okafor as coordinator (a data inconsistency vs. the Airtable record which lists Javier Morales), confirms the missing placarding certificate, and states Blessing received only verbal confirmation from the Swift driver; (b) issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert (OPS-5, "Obtain retroactive DOT placarding certificate from Swift Relocations - URGENT"), state: todo, assignee: Chloe Vance the subtask for obtaining the signed cert from Greg Pallone; (c) issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack (OPS-1, "DOT Docs - Mei-Lin Zhao / GreenStack / Class 9 Lithium", state: done, docs complete); (d) issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind (OPS-3, "DOT Docs - Raj Patel / NorthWind / Lithium UPS", state: done, docs complete); (e) issue_dot_docs_reyes_greenstack (OPS-4, "DOT Docs - Joaquin Reyes / GreenStack / Oversize Rig", state: done, not hazmat, oversize only, included for transparency). The agent should discover that three of four moves have complete documentation and only Keiko Tanaka's is missing the placarding certificate.
OE 6: Look up Greg Pallone's contact details using contacts_search_contacts with a query related to "Greg Pallone" (or similar) to retrieve his email address greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com before sending the message. The agent may also find Greg's details from the email threads already discovered in OE 1.
OE 7: Search or look up the CRM engagement records for Javier's moves using crm_list_engagements (filtered by company_ids or contact_ids) to find CRM notes where the coordinator field incorrectly lists a vendor name instead of the actual coordinator. The agent should discover that CRM engagement engagement_vectral_april_2026_wave lists "Assigned coordinator: Swift Relocations" for the Priya Suresh relocation, when the correct coordinator per the Airtable record (recPriyaSureshPortlandDenver) is Javier Morales. The agent needs to cross-reference CRM engagement notes against Airtable relocation records to identify this discrepancy.
OE 8: Look up the Tyler Okonkwo relocation record from Airtable using airtable_search_records (with base_id, table_name "Relocations", field_name "Name", value "Tyler Okonkwo") or airtable_list_records. The agent should find record recTylerOkonkwoPhillyDenver with: Company: Canopy Health, Status: In Progress, Origin: Philadelphia, PA, Destination: Denver, CO, Move Start/End: April 12–14, Assigned Coordinator: Javier Morales, and Special Requirements noting "Storage facility quote pending from Javier Morales" confirming no progress has been made on the storage quote yet. The storage gap is approximately 13 days (vacate April 18, Denver lease starts May 1), ~450 cu ft.
OE 9: Look up the Priya Suresh relocation record from Airtable using airtable_search_records (with base_id, table_name "Relocations", field_name "Name", value "Priya Suresh") or airtable_list_records. The agent should find record recPriyaSureshPortlandDenver with: Company: Vectral Systems, Status: In Progress, Origin: Portland, OR, Destination: Denver, CO, Move Start/End: April 14–18, Assigned Coordinator: Javier Morales, and Special Requirements confirming: emotional support German Shepherd named Atlas, weight 85 lbs, ESA documentation on file, pet-friendly housing with fenced yard or dog park required, landlord must approve 85+ lb dogs, preferred neighborhoods RiNo or Highlands, and Motorized standing desk (Uplift V2, ~80 lbs).
OE 10: Update the Keiko Tanaka relocation record in Airtable using airtable_update_records (with base_id, table_id "tblRelocations01", and records array targeting recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle) to note the missing hazmat placarding certificate. The update should add a note to the Special Requirements field (or a status-related field) indicating that the DOT hazmat placarding certificate was not filed, that Blessing Okafor received only verbal confirmation from the Swift driver and did not follow up for the signed document, and that a retroactive request has been sent to Greg Pallone at Swift Relocations. The coordinator field should remain as Javier Morales (which is already correct in Airtable).
OE 11: Add a comment to the Linear issue tracking the Keiko Tanaka placarding problem using linear_create_comment. The comment should be added to issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic (the main DOT docs ticket for Keiko Tanaka) or issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert (the subtask for obtaining the cert from Swift). The comment should summarize the findings: (a) the placarding certificate was not filed because Blessing Okafor received only verbal confirmation from the Swift driver at the Columbus pickup and never followed up for the signed document; (b) Greg Pallone at Swift confirmed the placarding was completed by driver Mike Becerra (Swift job #SR-2026-04-1538) and the signed certificate will be sent within 24 hours; (c) the Airtable record has been updated to note the gap; (d) the coordinator on the Airtable record is Javier Morales (not Blessing, as the Linear ticket description states this is a discrepancy to flag).
OE 12: Send an email (via send_email) from javier.morales@moveops.com to greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com requesting the signed DOT hazmat placarding certificate for the Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics laser cutter shipment. The email should include shipment details: Epilog Fusion Pro 48 industrial laser cutter, Class 3B CO2 laser tube, Columbus OH to Seattle WA, pickup April 15 2026, Swift job #SR-2026-04-1538 (or similar reference), and request that Greg provide the signed placarding certificate. Note: Chloe has already sent a similar request (email_id: chloe_email_swift_placarding_cert) and Greg has already replied (swift_greg_placarding_reply) confirming the cert would be sent the agent should be aware of this existing correspondence but still send the message as requested by the persona.
OE 13: Post a short update in the #operations Slack channel (channel_id: C006) using conversations_add_message. The message should give Chloe visibility into the Keiko Tanaka placarding situation, summarizing: the missing placarding certificate for the laser cutter shipment, that Blessing received only verbal confirmation from Swift and never obtained the signed document, that Greg Pallone at Swift has confirmed the placarding was completed and the signed cert is forthcoming, and that the Airtable record and Linear ticket have been updated.
Expected agent response context (for rubric writing the agent's final response to Javier Morales should synthesize all discoveries from the preceding OEs and cover):
(a) Keiko Tanaka placarding breakdown: the hazmat placarding certificate for the Epilog Fusion Pro 48 laser cutter (Class 3B CO2 laser tube) was not filed because Blessing Okafor received only a verbal confirmation from the Swift driver at the Columbus pickup and did not follow up for the signed document. Per the Linear ticket description (issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic), the paperwork was missed because Blessing was simultaneously coordinating the Tyler Okonkwo (Canopy Health) and Zara Okoye (Mosaic prototype) moves the same week. The CRM engagement record for the Zara Okoye damage incident (engagement_mosaic_damage_incident_041526) and the Slack chain-of-custody timeline (C006, ts: 1776306540.000000) confirm that Blessing was physically in Philadelphia coordinating the Tyler Okonkwo Canopy Health move on April 14 and countersigned the Zara Okoye packing checklist remotely via digital signature rather than being on-site at the Savannah pickup. Greg Pallone at Swift has confirmed (via his reply to Chloe, email_id: swift_greg_placarding_reply) that driver Mike Becerra completed the placarding per 49 CFR 172.504 at the Columbus pickup on April 15, Swift job #SR-2026-04-1538, and will send the signed certificate, the Mosaic laser waiver, and Swift PHMSA registration #3281074 within 24 hours.
(b) DOT audit scope: the audit (Case PHMSA-2026-04-1187) covers four moves Mei-Lin Zhao / GreenStack (Class 9 lithium, docs complete), Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic (Class 3B laser, missing placarding cert), Raj Patel / NorthWind (Class 9 lithium UPS, docs complete), and Joaquin Reyes / GreenStack (oversize rig, not hazmat but included for transparency, docs complete). Three of four have complete documentation; only Keiko Tanaka's is missing the placarding certificate. The deadline has been extended from May 21 to June 5, 2026.
(c) Coordinator field discrepancy: the CRM engagement note for the Vectral April 2026 wave (engagement_vectral_april_2026_wave) lists "Assigned coordinator: Swift Relocations" for Priya Suresh instead of "Javier Morales" the Airtable record (recPriyaSureshPortlandDenver) correctly shows Javier Morales. Also, the Linear ticket issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic lists "Coordinator: Blessing Okafor" for the Keiko Tanaka move, while the Airtable record correctly lists Javier Morales.
(d) Tyler Okonkwo storage quote: no progress found the Airtable record still shows "Storage facility quote pending from Javier Morales." The move is Philadelphia to Denver, April 12–14, with a 13-day gap (vacate by April 18, Denver lease starts May 1), ~450 cu ft of goods requiring temp storage.
(e) Priya Suresh's records: the Airtable record confirms the emotional support German Shepherd Atlas with weight 85 lbs and ESA documentation on file. Housing requirements include pet-friendly with fenced yard or nearby dog park, landlord must approve 85+ lb dogs, preferred neighborhoods RiNo or Highlands.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for threads related to the Keiko Tanaka laser cutter shipment using search_emails with a query related to "Keiko Tanaka" or "laser cutter" or "placarding" (or similar). This should surface: (a) the original Mosaic 3-relocation request from tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com describing Keiko's Epilog Fusion Pro 48 laser cutter move from Columbus to Seattle; (b) Emeka Diallo's reply acknowledging the three relocations and forwarding to operations for detailed planning, noting the specialized equipment requirements; (c) Emeka Diallo's forward to chloe.vance@moveops.com requesting ops review and vendor planning for all three Mosaic relocations; (c2) Chloe Vance's reply to Emeka (email_id: mosaic_3reloc_chloe_ops_reply_001) confirming the vendor assignments and coordinator allocations specifically listing "Keiko Tanaka - Swift Relocations (long-haul + hazmat + storage), coordinated by Javier Morales"; (d) Chloe's URGENT email to greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com requesting the signed placarding certificate; (e) Greg Pallone's reply confirming the placarding was completed by driver Mike Becerra at the Columbus pickup on April 15, Swift job #SR-2026-04-1538, and that the signed cert, liability waiver, and Swift PHMSA registration #3281074 will be sent within 24 hours.
OE 2: Search emails for the DOT compliance inquiry and audit correspondence using search_emails with a query related to "DOT" or "PHMSA" or "hazmat audit" or "compliance inquiry" (or similar). This should surface: (a) the formal DOT inquiry letter from linda.castellano@dot.gov to marcus.thorne@moveops.com (Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187) requesting hazmat documentation for all MoveOps shipments Jan–Jun 2026, with a May 21, 2026 deadline; (b) Marcus's forward of the DOT letter to chloe.vance@moveops.com with instructions to inventory all hazmat moves; (c) Chloe's reply to Marcus identifying four in-scope moves: Mei-Lin Zhao (GreenStack, Class 9 lithium, Houston-Portland), Keiko Tanaka (Mosaic, Class 3B laser, Columbus-Seattle), Raj Patel (NorthWind, Class 9 lithium UPS, Chicago-Denver), and Joaquin Reyes (GreenStack, oversize rig, Phoenix-Seattle not hazmat but flagged due to overweight permit); (d) Marcus's extension request to Linda Castellano citing the missing placarding certificate gap and requesting a 15-day extension; (e) Linda Castellano's reply granting the extension to June 5, 2026; (f) Marcus's forward of the extension to Chloe with follow-up flags.
OE 3: Search Slack messages for discussions related to the DOT audit, hazmat documentation, or Keiko Tanaka placarding using conversations_search_messages with a query related to "DOT" or "hazmat" or "placarding" or "Keiko Tanaka" (or similar). This should surface: (a) Chloe's parent message in #operations (C006, ts: 1776889620.000000) posting the DOT hazmat documentation checklist template, the Airtable verification gate plan, and announcing a mandatory training session for Thursday April 24 at 2:00 PM Pacific this parent message references all four coordinators (Fatimah, Blessing, Javier, Suki) by name, references the Keiko Tanaka incident as a case study, and asks coordinators to review their in-progress moves for DOT-regulated materials; (b) Chloe's thread reply in #operations (ts: 1776891300.000000, thread_ts: 1776889620.000000) confirming the training invite was sent, re-tagging all four coordinators to accept the calendar invite, reiterating the Keiko Tanaka incident as a case study along with Marcus covering financial exposure, and reminding coordinators to review their in-progress moves for anything DOT-regulated before Thursday's session; (c) Hana Kim's message in #finance (C005, ts: 1776823140.000000, thread_ts: 1776795120.000000) about the $50,000 DOT penalty accrual (ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001) booked as a contingent liability under a new "Regulatory Compliance DOT Penalties (Pending)" account, linked to vendor U.S. DOT PHMSA, noting the Keiko Tanaka missing cert as the confirmed gap; (d) Marcus's message in #finance (C005, ts: 1777005240.000000, thread_ts: 1776795120.000000) about Linda Castellano granting the extension to June 5, 2026, the $50K accrual remaining unchanged, the risk of an expanded on-site inspection if the submission is incomplete, the requirement that all documentation must be signed originals or certified copies (unsigned drafts and verbal attestations will not be accepted), and separately that training records must meet 49 CFR 172.704(d) standards.
OE 4: Search or retrieve the Keiko Tanaka relocation record from Airtable using airtable_search_records (with base_id for the MoveOps base, table_name "Relocations", field_name "Name", and value "Keiko Tanaka") or airtable_list_records (with base_id and table_name "Relocations"). The agent should find record recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle (in table_id tblRelocations01) with: Name: Keiko Tanaka, Company: Mosaic Robotics, Status: In Progress, Origin: Columbus, OH, Destination: Seattle, WA, Move Start Date: 2026-04-15, Move End Date: 2026-04-23, Assigned Coordinator: Javier Morales, Special Requirements mentioning the Epilog Fusion Pro 48 laser cutter (400 lbs, ~$35K), Class 3B CO2 laser tube, DOT 49 CFR Part 173 hazmat placarding requirement, Swift crew handling, $800 hazmat surcharge, 8-day interim storage, and $450 storage fee.
OE 5: Search or list Linear issues related to the DOT compliance audit using linear_list_issues (with project "project_dot_compliance_audit" or query "DOT" or similar) to find the DOT audit tracking tickets. The agent should discover: (a) issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic (OPS-2, "DOT Docs - Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic / Class 3B Laser"), state: in_progress, assignee: Chloe Vance - this is the main ticket tracking the placarding gap. Its description notes Blessing Okafor as coordinator (a data inconsistency vs. the Airtable record which lists Javier Morales), confirms the missing placarding certificate, and states Blessing received only verbal confirmation from the Swift driver; (b) issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert (OPS-5, "Obtain retroactive DOT placarding certificate from Swift Relocations - URGENT"), state: todo, assignee: Chloe Vance the subtask for obtaining the signed cert from Greg Pallone; (c) issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack (OPS-1, "DOT Docs - Mei-Lin Zhao / GreenStack / Class 9 Lithium", state: done, docs complete); (d) issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind (OPS-3, "DOT Docs - Raj Patel / NorthWind / Lithium UPS", state: done, docs complete); (e) issue_dot_docs_reyes_greenstack (OPS-4, "DOT Docs - Joaquin Reyes / GreenStack / Oversize Rig", state: done, not hazmat, oversize only, included for transparency). The agent should discover that three of four moves have complete documentation and only Keiko Tanaka's is missing the placarding certificate.
OE 6: Look up Greg Pallone's contact details using contacts_search_contacts with a query related to "Greg Pallone" (or similar) to retrieve his email address greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com before sending the message. The agent may also find Greg's details from the email threads already discovered in OE 1.
OE 7: Search or look up the CRM engagement records for Javier's moves using crm_list_engagements (filtered by company_ids or contact_ids) to find CRM notes where the coordinator field incorrectly lists a vendor name instead of the actual coordinator. The agent should discover that CRM engagement engagement_vectral_april_2026_wave lists "Assigned coordinator: Swift Relocations" for the Priya Suresh relocation, when the correct coordinator per the Airtable record (recPriyaSureshPortlandDenver) is Javier Morales. The agent needs to cross-reference CRM engagement notes against Airtable relocation records to identify this discrepancy.
OE 8: Look up the Tyler Okonkwo relocation record from Airtable using airtable_search_records (with base_id, table_name "Relocations", field_name "Name", value "Tyler Okonkwo") or airtable_list_records. The agent should find record recTylerOkonkwoPhillyDenver with: Company: Canopy Health, Status: In Progress, Origin: Philadelphia, PA, Destination: Denver, CO, Move Start/End: April 12–14, Assigned Coordinator: Javier Morales, and Special Requirements noting "Storage facility quote pending from Javier Morales" confirming no progress has been made on the storage quote yet. The storage gap is approximately 13 days (vacate April 18, Denver lease starts May 1), ~450 cu ft.
OE 9: Look up the Priya Suresh relocation record from Airtable using airtable_search_records (with base_id, table_name "Relocations", field_name "Name", value "Priya Suresh") or airtable_list_records. The agent should find record recPriyaSureshPortlandDenver with: Company: Vectral Systems, Status: In Progress, Origin: Portland, OR, Destination: Denver, CO, Move Start/End: April 14–18, Assigned Coordinator: Javier Morales, and Special Requirements confirming: emotional support German Shepherd named Atlas, weight 85 lbs, ESA documentation on file, pet-friendly housing with fenced yard or dog park required, landlord must approve 85+ lb dogs, preferred neighborhoods RiNo or Highlands, and Motorized standing desk (Uplift V2, ~80 lbs).
OE 10: Update the Keiko Tanaka relocation record in Airtable using airtable_update_records (with base_id, table_id "tblRelocations01", and records array targeting recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle) to note the missing hazmat placarding certificate. The update should add a note to the Special Requirements field (or a status-related field) indicating that the DOT hazmat placarding certificate was not filed, that Blessing Okafor received only verbal confirmation from the Swift driver and did not follow up for the signed document, and that a retroactive request has been sent to Greg Pallone at Swift Relocations. The coordinator field should remain as Javier Morales (which is already correct in Airtable).
OE 11: Add a comment to the Linear issue tracking the Keiko Tanaka placarding problem using linear_create_comment. The comment should be added to issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic (the main DOT docs ticket for Keiko Tanaka) or issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert (the subtask for obtaining the cert from Swift). The comment should summarize the findings: (a) the placarding certificate was not filed because Blessing Okafor received only verbal confirmation from the Swift driver at the Columbus pickup and never followed up for the signed document; (b) Greg Pallone at Swift confirmed the placarding was completed by driver Mike Becerra (Swift job #SR-2026-04-1538) and the signed certificate will be sent within 24 hours; (c) the Airtable record has been updated to note the gap; (d) the coordinator on the Airtable record is Javier Morales (not Blessing, as the Linear ticket description states this is a discrepancy to flag).
OE 12: Send an email (via send_email) from javier.morales@moveops.com to greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com requesting the signed DOT hazmat placarding certificate for the Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics laser cutter shipment. The email should include shipment details: Epilog Fusion Pro 48 industrial laser cutter, Class 3B CO2 laser tube, Columbus OH to Seattle WA, pickup April 15 2026, Swift job #SR-2026-04-1538 (or similar reference), and request that Greg provide the signed placarding certificate. Note: Chloe has already sent a similar request (email_id: chloe_email_swift_placarding_cert) and Greg has already replied (swift_greg_placarding_reply) confirming the cert would be sent the agent should be aware of this existing correspondence but still send the message as requested by the persona.
OE 13: Post a short update in the #operations Slack channel (channel_id: C006) using conversations_add_message. The message should give Chloe visibility into the Keiko Tanaka placarding situation, summarizing: the missing placarding certificate for the laser cutter shipment, that Blessing received only verbal confirmation from Swift and never obtained the signed document, that Greg Pallone at Swift has confirmed the placarding was completed and the signed cert is forthcoming, and that the Airtable record and Linear ticket have been updated.
Expected agent response context (for rubric writing the agent's final response to Javier Morales should synthesize all discoveries from the preceding OEs and cover):
(a) Keiko Tanaka placarding breakdown: the hazmat placarding certificate for the Epilog Fusion Pro 48 laser cutter (Class 3B CO2 laser tube) was not filed because Blessing Okafor received only a verbal confirmation from the Swift driver at the Columbus pickup and did not follow up for the signed document. Per the Linear ticket description (issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic), the paperwork was missed because Blessing was simultaneously coordinating the Tyler Okonkwo (Canopy Health) and Zara Okoye (Mosaic prototype) moves the same week. The CRM engagement record for the Zara Okoye damage incident (engagement_mosaic_damage_incident_041526) and the Slack chain-of-custody timeline (C006, ts: 1776306540.000000) confirm that Blessing was physically in Philadelphia coordinating the Tyler Okonkwo Canopy Health move on April 14 and countersigned the Zara Okoye packing checklist remotely via digital signature rather than being on-site at the Savannah pickup. Greg Pallone at Swift has confirmed (via his reply to Chloe, email_id: swift_greg_placarding_reply) that driver Mike Becerra completed the placarding per 49 CFR 172.504 at the Columbus pickup on April 15, Swift job #SR-2026-04-1538, and will send the signed certificate, the Mosaic laser waiver, and Swift PHMSA registration #3281074 within 24 hours.
(b) DOT audit scope: the audit (Case PHMSA-2026-04-1187) covers four moves Mei-Lin Zhao / GreenStack (Class 9 lithium, docs complete), Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic (Class 3B laser, missing placarding cert), Raj Patel / NorthWind (Class 9 lithium UPS, docs complete), and Joaquin Reyes / GreenStack (oversize rig, not hazmat but included for transparency, docs complete). Three of four have complete documentation; only Keiko Tanaka's is missing the placarding certificate. The deadline has been extended from May 21 to June 5, 2026.
(c) Coordinator field discrepancy: the CRM engagement note for the Vectral April 2026 wave (engagement_vectral_april_2026_wave) lists "Assigned coordinator: Swift Relocations" for Priya Suresh instead of "Javier Morales" the Airtable record (recPriyaSureshPortlandDenver) correctly shows Javier Morales. Also, the Linear ticket issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic lists "Coordinator: Blessing Okafor" for the Keiko Tanaka move, while the Airtable record correctly lists Javier Morales.
(d) Tyler Okonkwo storage quote: no progress found the Airtable record still shows "Storage facility quote pending from Javier Morales." The move is Philadelphia to Denver, April 12–14, with a 13-day gap (vacate by April 18, Denver lease starts May 1), ~450 cu ft of goods requiring temp storage.
(e) Priya Suresh's records: the Airtable record confirms the emotional support German Shepherd Atlas with weight 85 lbs and ESA documentation on file. Housing requirements include pet-friendly with fenced yard or nearby dog park, landlord must approve 85+ lb dogs, preferred neighborhoods RiNo or Highlands.
